examples.english: His throne extends over the heavens and the earth

senses.gloss: chair for lectures, lectern, pulpit, cathedra, a kind of desk upon which a document is placed
gun carriage, the mounting of a mangonel

etymology.text:
Etymology tree
Aramaic כּוּרְסְיָא (kursəyā)bor.
Classical Syriac ܟܘܪܣܝܐ (kursəyā)bor.
Arabic كرسي
Deemed with such a shape borrowed from Aramaic כּוּרְסְיָא / ܟܘܪܣܝܐ (kursəyā). Compare Biblical Hebrew כִּסֵּא (kisséʔ), Ugaritic 𐎋𐎒𐎜 (ksủ /⁠kussīʾu⁠/) and Akkadian 𒄑𒄖𒍝 (kussûm). Ultimately from Sumerian 𒄑𒄖𒍝 (^(g̃eš)gu-za /⁠guza⁠/, “chair”).
